title: CD Projekt Red admits losing the trust of some fans after Cyberpunk 2077's launch
summary: The executives of the Polish studio admit that the disastrous release of the game damaged the relationship with part of its community, possibly permanently.

title: Claude Guillemot, co-founder of Ubisoft, dies in a plane crash
summary: Claude Guillemot, one of the founding brothers of the French company, died when his plane crashed.

title: Xbox co-founder anticipated Microsoft's current problems in the sector 25 years ago
summary: The executive points out that her initial criticisms about the company's strategy are coming true over time.

title: Bleach announces the Hell arc and a definitive date for its long-awaited ending after 22 years
summary: Tite Kubo's franchise prepares new animation that could close the main story and explore the Hell Arc.

title: Aardman's Pokémon series shows its first poster
summary: The stop-motion studio behind Wallace and Gromit reveals the first image of its animated project set in the Pokémon world.

title: Obsidian faces a class-action lawsuit over alleged wage violations
summary: The RPG developer studio, a subsidiary of Microsoft, is accused of violating labor laws in California.

title: Former Unreal Engine evangelist leaves Epic Games and reflects on the industry's pivotal moment
summary: Sjoerd De Jong leaves the company after years of promoting the graphics engine, urging the industry to reconsider its direction.

title: Don't Starve Together sets a new concurrent player record ten years after its release
summary: Klei's survival game continues to attract players, surpassing its previous peak of connected users.

title: Stigma against AI reduces a game's reviews on Steam by up to 53%
summary: A data analysis reveals that titles labeled as generated by artificial intelligence receive fewer reviews and more negative ones.

title: Possible GTA 6 prices cause concern among fans
summary: Rumors about a high cost for Rockstar's next installment provoke debate in the community.

title: Valorant launches a new map and mode in its 2026 Act 4 season
summary: Riot Games' tactical shooter renews its offer with new content that promises to change the game dynamics.

title: Poppy Playtime director accumulates 23,000 hours on Steam to understand the market
summary: Bryce Clark assures that his addiction to the platform has served him to analyze trends and improve his games.

title: CD Projekt Red believes that games created entirely with AI are 'imminent'
summary: The head of the Polish studio believes that AI will generate complete titles, but questions if it's the right path.

title: The Last of Us Part 2 will receive an unofficial multiplayer mode developed by modders
summary: A group of fans is creating an online mode for the game, available on PC but not on PlayStation consoles.

title: Crunchyroll will stream the French animated film 'The Wolf', based on the Snowpiercer comic
summary: The streaming platform adds to its catalog an adaptation of Jacques Lob's comic, with French animation.

title: Kabam, studio of Marvel Contest of Champions, lays off staff without revealing numbers
summary: The company consolidates its office in Los Angeles, causing the departure of several employees.

title: Malware alert on Steam: Wallpaper Engine can be used to steal accounts
summary: A cybersecurity company warns that certain animated wallpapers contain malicious software that compromises users' accounts.

title: Microsoft eases the launch of Xbox games on PC to match Steam
summary: New developer tools aim to simplify publishing on Microsoft's store for computers.

title: Nintendo Life celebrates Sonic the Hedgehog with several specials for his 35th anniversary
summary: The website dedicates to the blue hedgehog a selection of favorite levels, a guide of the best games, and a survey on covers.

title: Naruto will have its first official trading card game in more than ten years
summary: Masashi Kishimoto's franchise returns to the TCG format with a new licensed proposal for fans.
